_comment: RMOOZ unit image asset manifest — provenance record for every locally cached image.

uss-lake-champlain-cvs39.jpg.subject: USS Lake Champlain (CVS-39)
uss-lake-champlain-cvs39.jpg.description: USS Lake Champlain (CVS-39) underway, February 1965. Essex-class anti-submarine aircraft carrier.
uss-lake-champlain-cvs39.jpg.license: Public Domain — U.S. Government work (17 U.S.C. § 105); no copyright applies.

meko.note: Naval combatant catalog default; Essex-class carrier used as representative naval asset.

uss-lake-champlain-cvs39.jpg.notes: Naval combatant / meko DB1 catalog default + specific unit-level image for USS Lake Champlain. Pass as unit.image_url in scenario to use ship-specific context.

patriot-sam-battery.jpg.subject: U.S. Army Patriot SAM Battery (MIM-104), Poland 2010
patriot-sam-battery.jpg.description: U.S. Army soldiers from Alpha Battery, 5-7 ADA instructing Polish forces on Patriot preventive maintenance in Morag, Poland, June 2010.
patriot-sam-battery.jpg.source_author: Staff Sergeant Lawree Roscoe Washington Jr., U.S. Army
patriot-sam-battery.jpg.credit: U.S. Army / SSG Washington (PATRIOT battery in Poland, 2010)
patriot-sam-battery.jpg.license: Public Domain — U.S. Army work created in official capacity (17 U.S.C. § 105); no copyright applies.

patriot.note: MIM-104 Patriot system actually depicted. Representative match for this catalog entry.

air_defense.note: Generic air-defense / SAM reference image. Depicts Patriot; used as visual placeholder for any air-defense unit.

sam_s300.note: Generic SAM placeholder. This is NOT an S-300 photo. Replace with actual S-300 imagery when available.

sam_s75.note: Generic SAM placeholder. This is NOT an S-75 Dvina photo. Replace with actual S-75 imagery when available.

patriot-sam-battery.jpg.notes: Patriot photo acceptable for 'patriot' catalog entry. Used as generic placeholder for other SAM/air-defense entries until platform-specific imagery is sourced.

offline_note: All images in this manifest are cached locally and do not require network access. For online use, source_url is the canonical public-domain reference.
